methodology.allergens: Essential-only: 16-tag vocabulary (Celery, Gluten, Crustaceans, Fish, Eggs, Lupin, Molluscs, Mustard, Nuts, Peanuts, Sesame, Soya, Sulphites, Sugared drink, Dairy, Lentils). Korean mapping: soy sauce/gochujang/doenjang -> Soya+Gluten; tofu/edamame/kongnamul -> Soya; sesame seeds/oil -> Sesame; anchovy/fish sauce/fish cake -> Fish; shrimp/shrimp paste -> Crustaceans; squid/octopus -> Molluscs. Traditional kimchi assumed to contain fish sauce + shrimp paste. Semi-finished components expanded before tag resolution. Bibimbap/Bulgogi/Japchae/Kimbap carry Sesame+Soya+Gluten base by category heuristic.
methodology.calories: Per-100g estimates applied to EP quantities (KG/L treated as ~1000g, GM/ML as grams). Egg pieces = 72 kcal each. SF components costed via weighted kcal of composition. Packaging excluded. Wastage not applied. Integers rounded. Drinks hardcoded (Coca Cola 139, Fanta 160, Sprite 140, zero-sugar ~1, water 0).

changelog.entry: Initial extraction from Korean Recipes.xlsx (Seoul Food brand, codes SEO101-SEO168) with SF expansion, allergen enrichment, and per-100g calorie estimates.
Drift fix: normalized ingredient/packaging shape to canonical 5-key {name, quantity, unit, wastage_pct, notes}.

pending_decisions: Confirm kimchi spec (default = traditional with fish sauce + shrimp paste). Flip to vegetarian removes Fish + Crustaceans tags on all kimchi-containing dishes.
Confirm whether rice cakes (tteok) used are 100% rice (no wheat). Default: no Gluten from tteok itself; Gluten comes from soy sauce/gochujang instead.
Verify chicken frying batter composition — if egg included, Donkkaseu-type dishes confirmed Eggs.
